The E⊝ values for the redox couple (M3+/M2+) shows that Mn2+ and Co2+ ions are the strongest oxidising agents in aqueous solution.

The ions Ti2+, V2+ and Cr2+ are strong reducing agents and will liberate hydrogen from a dilute acid.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The electrochemical cell has cathode:
$Bi_2O_3 + 3H_2O + 6e^- \rightarrow 2Bi + 6OH^-$ with $E° = -0.44$ V
and anode:
$Sn(OH)_6^{2-} - 2e^- \rightarrow HSnO_2^- + H_2O + OH^-$ with $E° = -0.90$ V.
The standard cell EMF is $E°_{cell} = -0.44 - (-0.90) = 0.46$ V.
Using the Nernst equation with $Q = 10^6$ and $n = 6$ electrons transferred:
$E_{cell} = 0.46 - \frac{0.059}{6} \log(10^6) = 0.46 - \frac{0.059 \times 6}{6} = 0.46 - 0.059 = 0.401$ V.
Expressing as $x \times 10^{-1}$ V:
$0.401 = 4.01 \times 10^{-1}$ V, so $x = 4$ (nearest integer).

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Zns+Sn2+aq⇌Zn2+aq+Sns

Given: 2.303RTF = 0.059 V

Ecell0 = 2.303RT2F log Kc

⇒ Ecell0 = 0.0592 log (1020)

⇒ Ecell0 = 0.0592 × 20

⇒ Ecell0 = 0.59 V

Ecell0 = ESn2+/Sn0 - EZn2+/Zn0

⇒ Ecell0 = ESn2+/Sn0 - (-0.76)

⇒ ESn2+/Sn0  = -0.76 + 059 = -0.17

⇒ ESn/Sn2+0 = 0.17 = 17 × 10-2

So, answer is 17.

solution:
If the partial pressure of NO and NO2 gas is taken as 1 bar, then Answer is 4, else the question is bonus.

NO3-+4H++3e-⟶NO+2H2O   ENO3-/NOo=0.96 V

NO3-+2H++e-⟶NO2+H2O   ENO3-/NO2o=0.79

Let HNO3=y⇒H+=y and NO3-=y 

for same thermodynamic tendency

ENO3-/NO=ENO3-/NO2

or, ENO3-/NOo-0.0593logPNOy×y4=ENO3-/NO2o-0.0591logPNO2y×y2

or, 0.96-0.0593logPN0y5=0.79-0.0591logPNO2y3

or, 0.17=-0.0591logPNO2y3+0.0593logPNOy5
0.17=-0.05911logPNO2y3+0.05913logPNOy5

0.17=-0.05913logPNO23y9+0.05913logPNOy5

0.17=0.05913logPNOy5-logPNO23y9

0.17=0.05913logPNOy5×y9PNO23

Assume PNO≃PNO2=1 bar

0.17×30.059=logy4=8.644

logy=8.6444

logy=2.161

y=102.1

∴2x=2×2.161=4.322

Answer (4)
